verdict_reasoning: STIM1 (Stromal Interaction Molecule 1) is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein resident primarily in the ER membrane, but it has a well-characterized stable pool at the plasma membrane. The N-terminal EF-hand domain faces the ER lumen (and extracellular space at the PM), while the C-terminus is cytoplasmic. At the PM, STIM1 is anchored as a type I TM protein with an exposed extracellular/luminal N-terminal domain accessible from the outside. This PM pool is genuine and stable, not merely transient recruitment. Surface biotinylation and antibody-binding studies confirm extracellular epitope accessibility on intact cells. It is a bona fide surface-accessible protein via its own TM anchor.
